tools.name: admin.disableOrganizationAdminAccount
tools.description: Disables an Amazon Web Services account within the Organization as the GuardDuty delegated administrator.

tools.name: admin.enableOrganizationAdminAccount
tools.description: Enables an Amazon Web Services account within the organization as the GuardDuty delegated administrator.

tools.name: admin.listOrganizationAdminAccounts
tools.description: Lists the accounts configured as GuardDuty delegated administrators.

tools.name: detector.acceptAdministratorInvitation
tools.description: Accepts the invitation to be a member account and get monitored by a GuardDuty administrator account that sent the invitation.

tools.name: detector.acceptInvitation
tools.description: Accepts the invitation to be monitored by a GuardDuty administrator account.

tools.name: detector.archiveFindings
tools.description:
Archives GuardDuty findings that are specified by the list of finding IDs.

Only the administrator account can archive findings. Member accounts don't have permission to archive findings from their accounts.

tools.name: detector.createDetector
tools.description:
Creates a single Amazon GuardDuty detector. A detector is a resource that represents the GuardDuty service. To start using GuardDuty, you must create a detector in each Region where you enable the service. You can have only one detector per account per Region. All data sources are enabled in a new detector by default.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.createFilter
tools.description: Creates a filter using the specified finding criteria. The maximum number of saved filters per Amazon Web Services account per Region is 100. For more information, see Quotas for GuardDuty.

tools.name: detector.createIpSet
tools.description: Creates a new IPSet, which is called a trusted IP list in the console user interface. An IPSet is a list of IP addresses that are trusted for secure communication with Amazon Web Services infrastructure and applications. GuardDuty doesn't generate findings for IP addresses that are included in IPSets. Only users from the administrator account can use this operation.

tools.name: detector.createMembers
tools.description:
Creates member accounts of the current Amazon Web Services account by specifying a list of Amazon Web Services account IDs. This step is a prerequisite for managing the associated member accounts either by invitation or through an organization.

When using Create Members as an organizations delegated administrator this action will enable GuardDuty in the added member accounts, with the exception of the organization delegated administrator account, which must enable GuardDuty prior to being added as a member.

If you are adding accounts by invitation, use this action after GuardDuty has bee enabled in potential member accounts and before using InviteMembers.

tools.name: detector.createPublishingDestination
tools.description: Creates a publishing destination to export findings to. The resource to export findings to must exist before you use this operation.

tools.name: detector.createSampleFindings
tools.description: Generates sample findings of types specified by the list of finding types. If 'NULL' is specified for findingTypes, the API generates sample findings of all supported finding types.

tools.name: detector.createThreatIntelSet
tools.description: Creates a new ThreatIntelSet. ThreatIntelSets consist of known malicious IP addresses. GuardDuty generates findings based on ThreatIntelSets. Only users of the administrator account can use this operation.

tools.name: detector.deleteDetector
tools.description: Deletes an Amazon GuardDuty detector that is specified by the detector ID.

tools.name: detector.deleteFilter
tools.description: Deletes the filter specified by the filter name.

tools.name: detector.deleteIpSet
tools.description: Deletes the IPSet specified by the ipSetId. IPSets are called trusted IP lists in the console user interface.

tools.name: detector.deleteMembers
tools.description:
Deletes GuardDuty member accounts (to the current GuardDuty administrator account) specified by the account IDs.

With autoEnableOrganizationMembers configuration for your organization set to ALL, you'll receive an error if you attempt to disable GuardDuty for a member account in your organization.

tools.name: detector.deletePublishingDestination
tools.description: Deletes the publishing definition with the specified destinationId.

tools.name: detector.deleteThreatIntelSet
tools.description: Deletes the ThreatIntelSet specified by the ThreatIntelSet ID.

tools.name: detector.describeMalwareScans
tools.description:
Returns a list of malware scans. Each member account can view the malware scans for their own accounts. An administrator can view the malware scans for all the member accounts.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.describeOrganizationConfiguration
tools.description:
Returns information about the account selected as the delegated administrator for GuardDuty.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.describePublishingDestination
tools.description: Returns information about the publishing destination specified by the provided destinationId.

tools.name: detector.disassociateFromAdministratorAccount
tools.description:
Disassociates the current GuardDuty member account from its administrator account.

With autoEnableOrganizationMembers configuration for your organization set to ALL, you'll receive an error if you attempt to disable GuardDuty in a member account.

tools.name: detector.disassociateFromMasterAccount
tools.description: Disassociates the current GuardDuty member account from its administrator account.

tools.name: detector.disassociateMembers
tools.description:
Disassociates GuardDuty member accounts (to the current administrator account) specified by the account IDs.

With autoEnableOrganizationMembers configuration for your organization set to ALL, you'll receive an error if you attempt to disassociate a member account before removing them from your Amazon Web Services organization.

tools.name: detector.getAdministratorAccount
tools.description: Provides the details for the GuardDuty administrator account associated with the current GuardDuty member account.

tools.name: detector.getCoverageStatistics
tools.description: Retrieves aggregated statistics for your account. If you are a GuardDuty administrator, you can retrieve the statistics for all the resources associated with the active member accounts in your organization who have enabled EKS Runtime Monitoring and have the GuardDuty agent running on their EKS nodes.

tools.name: detector.getDetector
tools.description:
Retrieves an Amazon GuardDuty detector specified by the detectorId.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.getFilter
tools.description: Returns the details of the filter specified by the filter name.

tools.name: detector.getFindings
tools.description: Describes Amazon GuardDuty findings specified by finding IDs.

tools.name: detector.getFindingsStatistics
tools.description: Lists Amazon GuardDuty findings statistics for the specified detector ID.

tools.name: detector.getIpSet
tools.description: Retrieves the IPSet specified by the ipSetId.

tools.name: detector.getMalwareScanSettings
tools.description:
Returns the details of the malware scan settings.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.getMasterAccount
tools.description: Provides the details for the GuardDuty administrator account associated with the current GuardDuty member account.

tools.name: detector.getMemberDetectors
tools.description:
Describes which data sources are enabled for the member account's detector.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.getMembers
tools.description: Retrieves GuardDuty member accounts (of the current GuardDuty administrator account) specified by the account IDs.

tools.name: detector.getRemainingFreeTrialDays
tools.description: Provides the number of days left for each data source used in the free trial period.

tools.name: detector.getThreatIntelSet
tools.description: Retrieves the ThreatIntelSet that is specified by the ThreatIntelSet ID.

tools.name: detector.getUsageStatistics
tools.description: Lists Amazon GuardDuty usage statistics over the last 30 days for the specified detector ID. For newly enabled detectors or data sources, the cost returned will include only the usage so far under 30 days. This may differ from the cost metrics in the console, which project usage over 30 days to provide a monthly cost estimate. For more information, see Understanding How Usage Costs are Calculated.

tools.name: detector.inviteMembers
tools.description: Invites other Amazon Web Services accounts (created as members of the current Amazon Web Services account by CreateMembers) to enable GuardDuty, and allow the current Amazon Web Services account to view and manage these accounts' findings on their behalf as the GuardDuty administrator account.

tools.name: detector.listCoverage
tools.description:
Lists coverage details for your GuardDuty account. If you're a GuardDuty administrator, you can retrieve all resources associated with the active member accounts in your organization.

Make sure the accounts have EKS Runtime Monitoring enabled and GuardDuty agent running on their EKS nodes.

tools.name: detector.listDetectors
tools.description: Lists detectorIds of all the existing Amazon GuardDuty detector resources.

tools.name: detector.listFilters
tools.description: Returns a paginated list of the current filters.

tools.name: detector.listFindings
tools.description: Lists Amazon GuardDuty findings for the specified detector ID.

tools.name: detector.listIpSets
tools.description: Lists the IPSets of the GuardDuty service specified by the detector ID. If you use this operation from a member account, the IPSets returned are the IPSets from the associated administrator account.

tools.name: detector.listMembers
tools.description: Lists details about all member accounts for the current GuardDuty administrator account.

tools.name: detector.listPublishingDestinations
tools.description: Returns a list of publishing destinations associated with the specified detectorId.

tools.name: detector.listThreatIntelSets
tools.description: Lists the ThreatIntelSets of the GuardDuty service specified by the detector ID. If you use this operation from a member account, the ThreatIntelSets associated with the administrator account are returned.

tools.name: detector.startMonitoringMembers
tools.description: Turns on GuardDuty monitoring of the specified member accounts. Use this operation to restart monitoring of accounts that you stopped monitoring with the StopMonitoringMembers operation.

tools.name: detector.stopMonitoringMembers
tools.description:
Stops GuardDuty monitoring for the specified member accounts. Use the StartMonitoringMembers operation to restart monitoring for those accounts.

With autoEnableOrganizationMembers configuration for your organization set to ALL, you'll receive an error if you attempt to stop monitoring the member accounts in your organization.

tools.name: detector.unarchiveFindings
tools.description: Unarchives GuardDuty findings specified by the findingIds.

tools.name: detector.updateDetector
tools.description:
Updates the Amazon GuardDuty detector specified by the detectorId.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.updateFilter
tools.description: Updates the filter specified by the filter name.

tools.name: detector.updateFindingsFeedback
tools.description: Marks the specified GuardDuty findings as useful or not useful.

tools.name: detector.updateIpSet
tools.description: Updates the IPSet specified by the IPSet ID.

tools.name: detector.updateMalwareScanSettings
tools.description:
Updates the malware scan settings.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.updateMemberDetectors
tools.description:
Contains information on member accounts to be updated.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.updateOrganizationConfiguration
tools.description:
Configures the delegated administrator account with the provided values. You must provide the value for either autoEnableOrganizationMembers or autoEnable.

There might be regional differences because some data sources might not be available in all the Amazon Web Services Regions where GuardDuty is presently supported. For more information, see Regions and endpoints.

tools.name: detector.updatePublishingDestination
tools.description: Updates information about the publishing destination specified by the destinationId.

tools.name: detector.updateThreatIntelSet
tools.description: Updates the ThreatIntelSet specified by the ThreatIntelSet ID.

tools.name: invitation.declineInvitations
tools.description: Declines invitations sent to the current member account by Amazon Web Services accounts specified by their account IDs.

tools.name: invitation.deleteInvitations
tools.description: Deletes invitations sent to the current member account by Amazon Web Services accounts specified by their account IDs.

tools.name: invitation.getInvitationsCount
tools.description: Returns the count of all GuardDuty membership invitations that were sent to the current member account except the currently accepted invitation.

tools.name: invitation.listInvitations
tools.description: Lists all GuardDuty membership invitations that were sent to the current Amazon Web Services account.

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists tags for a resource. Tagging is currently supported for detectors, finding filters, IP sets, and threat intel sets, with a limit of 50 tags per resource. When invoked, this operation returns all assigned tags for a given resource.

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description: Adds tags to a resource.

tools.name: tags.untagResource
tools.description: Removes tags from a resource.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
